summary: Phase B Alpha Decay Chart — Per-Trade Forward Return Distribution Tracker. All three sub-features landed and tests pass 55/55.

changes.description: Added 1-hour disk cache (data/alpha_decay_cache.json). Cache key is (closed-trade count | latest entry date) so a new trade arriving automatically invalidates. Cache is only used for file-sourced calls — explicit-trades callers (tests, backtest) bypass it. Tab switches are now instant after the first load.

Three UI improvements: (1) T+3 column added to Segment Breakdown table alongside T+1, T+5, T+10 — all four horizons from the research spec are now visible. (2) 'All / Regime' vs 'By Dimension' toggle button pair above the chart — the dimension view plots one line per active signal type (Trend, Momentum, Squeeze, Flow, Breakout, MTF, News, Social, Reversion), enabling half-life comparison per signal type. (3) Optimal-horizon vertical dashed line (yellow) drawn directly on the chart canvas via an inline Chart.js plugin; the x-axis tick for that horizon also renders in yellow, making the exit sweet-spot immediately obvious.

Added TestCaching class (11 new tests): cache key stability, closed-trade-only counting, save/load roundtrip, wrong-key miss, missing-file miss, TTL expiry, stats cache-hit integration, explicit-trades bypass. Fixed 4 pre-existing tests that mock compute_alpha_decay without patching the cache — they now also patch _load_cache/_save_cache/_TRADE_LOG_FILE so mock return values are actually used.

research_basis: Timothy Masters / markrbest.github.io: alpha decay charts measure return distribution over a trade's lifetime. A clean upward trajectory from entry beats a dip-and-recover even if final PnL is identical.
MicroAlphas (2025): swing-trade signal effectiveness diminishes after 3–10 days. Optimal exit identified by peak of the median forward-return curve before mean-reversion inflects.
paper_trade_path: Dashboard → Alpha tab → alpha_decay API endpoint → get_alpha_decay_stats() (caching applied). Verified module imports cleanly. yfinance fetches are unchanged; cache makes subsequent tab switches instant.
